decision_summary: The chip max temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest priority lever to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the peak temperature.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 20% (within the 2.0x limit) improves lateral heat distribution, reducing the thermal bottleneck at the chip interface.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the base plate conductivity becomes the new bottleneck
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications (not modeled here)
